title: Tragacantha puberula
intro: Tragacantha puberula, also known as Astragalus puberulus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, gastrointestinal problems, and infections, due to its purported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha puberula include alkaloids, flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Tragacantha puberula is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or dried extract, and is commonly used to make teas, soups, and capsules for its perceived health benefits.
taxonomy: Tragacantha puberula is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is commonly found in dry areas and has a hairy appearance due to its puberulent foliage. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The exact classification of Tragacantha puberula may vary depending on the source, but it is often considered a member of the subfamily Faboideae. Further research may be needed to confirm its precise classification within the plant kingdom.
